name: OmniFocus MCP Server
what_it_does: OmniFocus MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with OmniFocus — the premium macOS/iOS GTD task manager — querying tasks, projects, and tags, creating and completing tasks, and integrating OmniFocus data into agent-driven productivity and task management workflows on Apple platforms.
use_cases: Querying OmniFocus tasks and projects from productivity agents
Creating new tasks and projects in OmniFocus from capture agents
Completing and flagging tasks from workflow automation agents
Reviewing due tasks and upcoming work from planning agents
Integrating OmniFocus with other tools from automation agents
GTD weekly review assistance from productivity coaching agents
not_for: Teams using Todoist, Things, or other task managers
best_when: A macOS power user with OmniFocus needs AI-assisted GTD task management — automating capture, review, and organization of their OmniFocus database.
avoid_when: You're not on macOS or don't use OmniFocus — use Todoist MCP or similar cross-platform alternatives.

auth.notes: No authentication — communicates with OmniFocus via AppleScript/JXA on macOS. OmniFocus must be installed and running. May require Accessibility permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: None — OmniFocus requires subscription or purchase
pricing.notes: OmniFocus is a premium macOS/iOS task manager. Community MCP server is free. OmniFocus license required.

performance.rate_limits: Local OmniFocus — no external rate limits; AppleScript speed dependent

known_agent_gotchas: OmniFocus must be running — does not launch OmniFocus automatically
macOS Accessibility permissions required for AppleScript automation
OmniFocus subscription required — major cost barrier for casual users
AppleScript API may change between OmniFocus major versions
Community MCP — limited maintenance and feature set
macOS only — no iOS or web access via this MCP
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local AppleScript automation. No network credentials. OmniFocus data stays local. Community MCP. Grant Accessibility permissions carefully.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local AppleScript. No credentials needed. Community MCP. Data stays on device.
